title: Wrong Umbrella
html:
The floor is cold through the hospital gown, and that's what Jiang Wenxu registers before anything else makes sense: bare concrete against the backs of his thighs, a draft from somewhere low, the thin scratch of institutional cotton against his shoulders. The gown is open at the back. He's sitting on a floor he doesn't recognize, in a garment he didn't put on, in a room he's never seen.

He gets to his feet and looks around.

The room is large and square, its walls painted a pale institutional green that has been flaking for years, long curling strips of it hanging from the corners. The windows are boarded from the outside, the planks warped badly enough that thin gray daylight cuts through the gaps in uneven lines across the cracked linoleum. A water stain occupies most of one ceiling corner and branches outward in slow dry channels. Metal bed frames line the far wall, stripped completely, the springs corroded to a dull rust-orange. A wheelchair sits overturned near the beds, one wheel missing, its footrest bent out at an angle that suggests a great deal of force applied at some point.

'Hospital,' he thinks. Specifically the psychiatric kind, given the bed configuration.

Something materializes in the center of his field of view, text hovering at a comfortable reading distance.

---

【Welcome, Chosen Participant.】

【Instance Initiating: Abandoned Psychiatric Ward.】

【Survival Probability: CALCULATING…】

---

He reads it once, then again. The text drifts sideways into his peripheral vision like a notification he hasn't dismissed and sits there, stubborn and semi-transparent. He tries swiping at it with two fingers. Nothing happens.
